title: ADR-0072 S5: task-level failure memory + quarantine — stop re-picking a poison todo after task_retry_ceiling instead of releasing it back to inbox root every cycle
summary: The system-level halt (S3) covers pipeline failures. S5 covers the softer TASK-level case: an inbox item that genuinely fails on task grounds (real build/audit defect) is today released back to .evolve/inbox root on every cycle failure (inboxmover.ReleaseCycleProcessing) with NO memory of why — so triage re-selects it forever. Give the inbox item a durable failure memory and quarantine it after failure_policy.thresholds.task_retry_ceiling, so the loop stops burning tokens on one poison task while continuing other work (never_stop preserved for the healthy backlog).
root_cause: internal/inboxmover has no per-item failure count; internal/phases/triage reads .evolve/inbox root fresh every cycle. The CyclesUnpicked counter in cyclestate/state.go is DEAD (never incremented — confirmed by the ADR-0072 architecture map). So a task-level poison todo has no cooldown or ceiling.
fix: (1) Inbox item gains a durable failureHistory: [{cycle, classification, verdict}] written by inboxmover.Promote/Release on a task-level failure. (2) When an item's failure count reaches failure_policy.thresholds.task_retry_ceiling (already in policy, default 2), inboxmover routes it to .evolve/inbox/quarantine/ (with the diagnostic) instead of back to root — triage stops selecting it. (3) Wire the dead CyclesUnpicked with real increment semantics OR replace it with the failureHistory count. (4) An operator command to list/release quarantined items (evolve inbox quarantine). Config-driven via the shipped failure_policy block; zero new flags.
acceptance: A task failing task_retry_ceiling times is routed to .evolve/inbox/quarantine/ and is NOT visible to the next cycle's triage (test).
Other inbox items keep flowing (the loop does not stop) — only the poison task is quarantined (test).
Quarantined item carries the failure diagnostic (why quarantined).
System-level halt (S3) still takes precedence over task quarantine when both apply.
go vet, -race on internal/inboxmover, no regression in inbox lifecycle tests.
notes: This is the task-level half of 'don't keep retrying the same todo'. The system-level half (halt on a broken pipeline) already shipped in d531fc32. Quarantine != halt: quarantine one task and keep going; halt stops the whole loop for a pipeline fault.
